body: memory_search returns 404: models/embedding-001 is not supported by Gemini API. Hardcoded model name is outdated. Update to text-embedding-004 or equivalent.
title: [Bug]: memory_search fails - Gemini embedding model unsupported

body:
# OpenClaw Memory Search Bug Report

**Issue:** Memory search (Gemini embeddings) completely broken due to wrong model name

**Severity:** Critical - affects all users with `memorySearch.enabled: true` and `provider: "gemini"`

## Symptoms
- Memory search fails with error: `API key not valid` (misleading — key IS valid, model name is wrong)

## Root Cause
OpenClaw is calling the **wrong Gemini embedding model name**.

**Current (broken):** `models/embedding-001`
**Correct:** `models/text-embedding-004` or `models/gemini-embedding-001`

## Suggested Fix
Update the Gemini embedding model name to one of:
- `models/text-embedding-004` (recommended, latest)
- `models/gemini-embedding-001`

## Impact
- Memory search is completely non-functional for Gemini provider
- Error message is misleading (says "API key not valid" when it's actually wrong model name)

## Workaround
Switch provider to OpenAI or disable memory search.

## Environment
- **OpenClaw version:** 2026.2.17
- **OS:** macOS (Darwin 25.3.0, arm64)

---

**Tested:** 2026-02-18
title: Memory search broken: wrong Gemini embedding model name

body:
## Bug Description

Dreaming phase scans complete successfully and write inline reports to daily memory files, but the **narrative pipeline** never fires. DREAMS.md stays blank (only template markers) despite gateway logs showing `dream diary entry written`.

## Environment

- **OpenClaw:** 2026.4.11 (769908e)
- **OS:** macOS 26.3 arm64
- **Node:** v25.6.1
- **Model:** zai/glm-5-turbo (reasoning enabled)
- **Memory plugin:** memory-core (default)
- **Memory search:** gemini / gemini-embedding-001

## Root Cause

In `dreaming-narrative-CmQl7hjr.js`, line 298:

```js
async function generateAndAppendDreamNarrative(params) {
    // ...
    if (params.data.snippets.length === 0 && !params.data.promotions?.length) return;
    // ...
}
```

The function bails out immediately because `data.snippets` and `data.promotions` are **always empty arrays** when passed from the phase pipeline.

Evidence from `phase-signals.json` (336 entries, all with empty data):

```json
{
  "key": "memory:memory/2026-04-11.md:5:5",
  "lightHits": 2,
  "remHits": 0,
  "lastLightAt": "2026-04-11T19:30:00.552Z"
}
```

No `snippets` or `promotions` fields — they simply are not being populated by the phase scan.

## What Works

- ✅ Phase scans run every 30 minutes (light + rem)
- ✅ Inline phase reports write to `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` (175-375 lines for light, 10-14 for rem)
- ✅ `phase-signals.json` accumulates entries with `lightHits`/`remHits`
- ✅ Short-term recall store accumulates (888 entries, functioning)
- ✅ Gateway log shows: `memory-core: dream diary entry written for {phase} phase`

## What Does NOT Work

- ❌ `generateAndAppendDreamNarrative` never fires (snippets=[] && promotions=[])
- ❌ DREAMS.md stays empty — only contains template markers:
  ```
  # Dream Diary
  <!-- openclaw:dreaming:diary:start -->
  <!-- openclaw:dreaming:diary:end -->
  ```

## Ambiguous Log Message

The log line `dream diary entry written for {phase} phase` is emitted by **both**:
1. `writeDailyDreamingPhaseBlock` (inline report — working)
2. `appendNarrativeEntry` (DREAMS.md narrative — never reached)

This makes debugging harder because the log suggests DREAMS.md is being written when it is not. Consider adding the write target path to the log message to distinguish the two.

## Reproduction

1. Enable memory-core with dreaming enabled (default config)
2. Wait for phase scans to run (every 30 min)
3. Observe gateway logs show `dream diary entry written`
4. Check DREAMS.md — still empty
5. Check `phase-signals.json` — all entries have no `snippets` or `promotions`

## Expected Behavior

Phase scan should populate `snippets` (candidate memory fragments) and/or `promotions` (promoted memories) in the data passed to `generateAndAppendDreamNarrative`, which would then write dream diary entries to DREAMS.md.
title: [Bug] Dreaming narrative never writes to DREAMS.md — snippets/promotions always empty
